evals.prompt: I'm building a blog with Tina4 in Python. I need a Post model with title, body, author_id, and published_at fields, plus routes for listing all posts, getting a single post, creating a post (authenticated), and a template that shows a post with its author name. Show me how to set this up.
evals.expected_output: Should provide a complete working setup: ORM model in src/orm/, routes in src/routes/, Frond template in src/templates/, and JWT auth middleware. Should use convention over config, auto-discovery, and smart response types.
expectations: Provides an ORM model with the correct fields placed in src/orm/
Creates route handlers for GET /posts, GET /posts/{id}, and POST /posts
Places routes in src/routes/ for auto-discovery
Includes JWT or middleware-based authentication on the create route
Provides a Frond/Twig template for displaying a post
Uses smart response types (returning objects auto-converts to JSON)
Shows the database connection string format from .env

evals.prompt: I need to add a contact form to my Tina4 PHP app. When someone submits the form, it should save the message to the database and send an email notification to admin@mysite.com. Show me the full implementation — the template with the form, the route handler, the ORM model, and the email sending.
evals.expected_output: Should provide complete working code in PHP: Frond template with form, POST route handler, ContactMessage ORM model, and Email sending. Should follow Tina4 PHP conventions (camelCase methods, PascalCase classes).
expectations: Provides a Frond template with an HTML form
Creates a POST route handler in PHP using Tina4 conventions
Defines a ContactMessage ORM model in PHP
Includes email sending using Tina4's built-in Email class
Uses PHP naming conventions (camelCase methods, PascalCase class)
Mentions or uses CSRF/form token protection
File placement follows convention (src/routes/, src/orm/, src/templates/)

evals.prompt: I want to build a real-time dashboard in Tina4 Ruby that shows live order counts updating via WebSocket. When a new order comes in through the API, all connected dashboard clients should see the count update instantly. Show me how.
evals.expected_output: Should provide WebSocket setup, an order API route that fires an event, a Frond template with live blocks or frond.js WebSocket integration, and the event listener that triggers the broadcast.
expectations: Sets up a WebSocket endpoint using Tina4's websocket decorator/DSL
Creates an order POST route that triggers an update
Uses Tina4's event system or direct WebSocket broadcast
Provides a Frond template with either live blocks or frond.js WebSocket client
Uses Ruby naming conventions (snake_case methods)
Shows working code that can be dropped into a Tina4 project
Mentions the connection string or .env configuration
